subject: Biology
title: The Nucleus: Command Center
description: A quick win: understand The Nucleus: Command Center.

hook.text: The nucleus holds nearly all of a cell's DNA, packed into 46 chromosomes in humans.

buildup.text: A double membrane called the nuclear envelope keeps DNA safe from the cytoplasm.

discovery.text: Inside, the nucleolus builds ribosomal RNA—parts needed for protein-making machines.

twist.text: Red blood cells eject their nucleus entirely to make room for more hemoglobin.

climax.text: The nucleus doesn't run the cell directly. It sends RNA messages that ribosomes translate.

punchline.text: It writes instructions. Others do the work.

quiz.question: What does the nucleolus inside the nucleus produce?
